Amended MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-603846 WITH CASE CGC-23-605799
Off Calendar Apr-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. TYLER CESAR, Esq. of Pierce Kavicioglu Espinosa & Cesar (tel:818-728-4999) for Morrow-Meadows Corporation.
Atty. CHLOE LOOMER, Esq. of Hugo Parker, LLP (tel:415-808-0300) for Cross-Defendant Gilbert Spray Coat, INC.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT LEE'S IMPERIAL WELDING, INC. Amended MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-23-603846 WITH CASE CGC-23-605799.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The "motion to consolidate case no. GCG-23-605799 with case no. CGC-23-603846" is granted.
The two cases involve the same hotel construction project. Three parties favor the motion - two subcontractors and the general contractor. Only the project owner opposes.
Much of a construction defect trial is consumed with factual background and no one suggests this litigation would be any different. Moreover, "trellis defects" are central to both cases. The opposition argues that two parties agree to trellis liability, but this would still leave common issues as to why the trellis was defective, what repairs were necessary, whether and what delays were caused by trellis defects, damages and so on. Such common questions of fact and/or law call for consolidation. (CCP 1048(a).) If the trial judge believes efficiency would be served by separate trials, she or he can so order.
The first-filed case shall be the lead case.

Counsel Chloe Loomer is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
